title: The Artemis II mission has ended. Where does NASA go from here?
description: "The work ahead is greater than the work behind us."

title: Four astronauts are back home after a daring ride around the Moon
description: "I can't imagine a better crew that just completed a perfect mission right now."

title: Californians sue over AI tool that records doctor visits
description: Plaintiffs say transcription tool processed confidential chats offsite.

title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
description: This ban took place after Claude's pricing changed for OpenClaw users last week.

title: New paper argues history, not mantle plume, powers Yellowstone
description: A now-vanished plate under North America may open the crust below Yellowstone.

title: F1 moves a step closer to fixing its 2026 hybrid problem
description: Algorithms, not drivers, are deciding how hard to accelerate, and that's no good.

title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
description: The new AI model is being heralded—and feared—as a hacker’s superweapon. Experts say its arrival is a wake-up call for developers who have long made security an afterthought.

title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
description: OpenAI ignored three warnings that a ChatGPT user was dangerous — including its own mass-casualty flag — while he stalked and harassed his ex-girlfriend, a new lawsuit alleges.

title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
description: SusHi Tech 2026 is zeroing in on four technology domains reshaping society: AI, Robotics, Resilience, and Entertainment. title: Suspect Arrested for Allegedly Throwing Molotov Cocktail at Sam Altman’s Home
description: An attacker allegedly threw a molotov cocktail at the OpenAI CEO’s residence before making threats outside of the startup’s headquarters.

title: This Startup Wants You to Pay Up to Talk With AI Versions of Human Experts
description: Onix is launching a “Substack of bots,” where digital twins of health and wellness influencers dispense advice 24/7. And maybe hawk their products.

title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
description: Is it the Department of Defense or the Department of War? The Gulf of Mexico or the Gulf of America? A vaccine—or an “individualized neoantigen treatment”? title: Last 24 hours: Save up to $500 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass
description: This is it. Tonight is your last chance to lock in savings of up to $500 for your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass. These discounts end at 11:59 p.m. PT.

title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release

title: AI Podcasters Really Want to Tell You How to Keep a Man Happy
description: Videos of fake relationship guru podcasters are reinforcing gender tropes and racking up millions of views, all the while driving sales to AI influencer schools.

title: Constellations
description: I. We had crash-landed on the planet. We were far from home. The spaceship could not be repaired, and the rescue beacon had failed. title: Meta’s New AI Asked for My Raw Health Data—and Gave Me Terrible Advice
description: Meta’s Muse Spark model offers to analyze users’ health data, including lab results. Beyond the obvious privacy risks, it’s not a capable stand-in for a real doctor.

title: ChatGPT finally offers $100/month Pro plan
description: OpenAI announced on Thursday something that power users have been asking for: a $100/month plan. Previously, subscriptions jumped from $20 to $200 per month.

title: Is fake grass a bad idea? The AstroTurf wars are far from over.
description: A rare warm spell in January melted enough snow to uncover Cornell University’s newest athletic field, built for field hockey. title: Understanding Convolutions on Graphs
description: Understanding the building blocks and design choices of graph neural networks.

title: A Gentle Introduction to Graph Neural Networks
description: What components are needed for building learning algorithms that leverage the structure and properties of graphs?

title: Distill Hiatus
description: After five years, Distill will be taking a break.

title: Adversarial Reprogramming of Neural Cellular Automata
description: Reprogramming Neural CA to exhibit novel behaviour, using adversarial attacks.

title: Weight Banding
description: Weights in the final layer of common visual models appear as horizontal bands. We investigate how and why.
